name: Mick Jagger
description: British singer and songwriter; vocalist of The Rolling Stones
abstract: Sir Michael Philip Jagger (born 26 July 1943) is an English musician, songwriter, and film producer known as the lead singer and founder member of the Rolling Stones. Jagger has co-written most of the Stones' songs with lead guitarist Keith Richards; their songwriting partnership is one of the most successful in rock music history.

works.t: I Just Want to Make Love to You
I Just Want to Make Love to You

works_created.t: You Can't Always Get What You Want

about_quotes.speaker: Luis Conte
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee narrates a story about a third party ('he') who played with Mick Jagger, but the interviewee himself does not claim any personal interaction or relationship with Mick Jagger.
about_quotes.doc_title: Luis Conte interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, November 24, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Lou Marini
about_quotes.evidence: here comes Saturday Night Live and Peter Tosh and Mick Jagger and me playing the solo. It was me. I didn’t realize — no wonder I knew the solo, I played it.
about_quotes.doc_title: Lou Marini interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 15, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Artie Shaw
about_quotes.evidence: I think the vast American audience is listening to Rod whatever his name is [Stewart] and Prince and Mick Jagger and Madonna.
about_quotes.doc_title: Artie Shaw — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Don Alias
about_quotes.evidence: producer called me up and he says “hey man he wants you to play percussion on this Mick Jagger record.” ... turns out that Mick, all they wanted was for me to play on a Syndrum, drum fills.
about_quotes.doc_title: Don Alias interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 6, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Marshall
about_quotes.evidence: My kids were, even my stepchildren were like totally into Mick Jagger and Paul Butterfield and all those.
about_quotes.doc_title: Eddie Marshall interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Francisco, California, August 8, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Pete Levin
about_quotes.evidence: someone like Mick Jagger would be sitting in a dark corner with glasses on trying to be as inconspicuous as possible, just kind of listening to the band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Pete Levin interviewed by Monk Rowe, Danbury, Connecticut, November 20, 2001
